title: Olympic Games
description: 15 questions about Olympic history, records, host cities, famous athletes, and unusual sports

questions.question: In which city were the first modern Olympic Games held in 1896?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece in 1896, organized by Pierre de Coubertin.

questions.question: Who has won the most Olympic gold medals of all time?
questions.explanation: Michael Phelps won 23 Olympic gold medals in swimming across five Olympic Games (2000-2016).

questions.question: Which sport was removed from the Olympics after the 1900 Games but is sometimes cited as the strangest Olympic event?
questions.explanation: Live pigeon shooting appeared at the 1900 Paris Olympics. Nearly 300 birds were killed, and the event was never held again.

questions.question: In which year were the Olympic Games cancelled due to World War I?
questions.explanation: The 1916 Summer Olympics, scheduled for Berlin, were cancelled due to World War I.

questions.question: Which athlete lit the Olympic cauldron at the 1996 Atlanta Games?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali, visibly affected by Parkinson's disease, lit the Olympic cauldron in one of the most memorable moments in Olympic history.

questions.question: How many consecutive Olympic Games did wrestler Aleksandr Karelin win gold before losing in the 2000 final?
questions.explanation: Karelin won gold in 1988, 1992, and 1996 (3 consecutive golds) before losing to Rulon Gardner in the 2000 Sydney final.

questions.question: Which country has hosted the most Summer Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: The United States has hosted the Summer Olympics four times: 1904 (St. Louis), 1932 (Los Angeles), 1984 (Los Angeles), and 2028 (Los Angeles).

questions.question: What unusual event was part of the Olympic program from 1900 to 1920?
questions.explanation: Tug of war was an official Olympic sport from 1900 to 1920, contested at five consecutive Games.

questions.question: Who was the first athlete to win gold medals at both the Summer and Winter Olympics?
questions.explanation: Eddie Eagan won gold in boxing (1920 Summer) and bobsled (1932 Winter), making him the first to win gold at both.

questions.question: Usain Bolt's world record 100m time set at the 2009 World Championships is:
questions.explanation: Usain Bolt set the world record of 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin.

questions.question: Which city will host the 2028 Summer Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: Los Angeles will host the 2028 Summer Olympics, its third time hosting the Games.

questions.question: What controversial event occurred at the 1972 Munich Olympics?
questions.explanation: Palestinian terrorists took 11 Israeli team members hostage; all hostages were killed during a failed rescue attempt.

questions.question: Which gymnast achieved the first perfect 10.0 score in Olympic history?
questions.explanation: Nadia Comaneci of Romania scored the first perfect 10.0 at the 1976 Montreal Olympics on the uneven bars.

questions.question: Which ancient Olympic event involved a combination of wrestling and boxing with almost no rules?
questions.explanation: Pankration was a brutal ancient Greek combat sport combining wrestling and striking, with only biting and eye-gouging prohibited.

questions.question: How many Olympic gold medals did Jesse Owens win at the 1936 Berlin Olympics?
questions.explanation: Jesse Owens won 4 gold medals (100m, 200m, long jump, 4x100m relay) at the 1936 Berlin Olympics, defying Nazi ideology.
